commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e13cf730e16a6625d91efb700e705a84a1ba41c6)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Telegram action-runtime regression test asserting durable `sendMessage` action delivery emits exactly one internal `message:sent` hook.

PR surface: Tests +91. Total +91 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: the added import points from `extensions/telegram` into `src/hooks/internal-hooks.js`, and the boundary checker disallows extension imports resolving to `src/**` outside `src/plugin-sdk/**`. The original Telegram hook gap no longer reproduces on current main because action `sendMessage` now goes through durable outbound delivery.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for the proof gate plus one boundary import fix; ClawSweeper repair should not be queued while required external real behavior proof is missing.

target.title: test(telegram): guard action send message:sent hook
target.body:
## Summary
- add a Telegram action-runtime regression test proving durable `sendMessage` emits exactly one internal `message:sent` event
- lock the existing source path to the canonical internal hook event shape consumed by message:sent file-hooks
- cover the action/operator Path-B surface so future refactors cannot silently bypass native `message:sent`

## Context
Klawbi live dist on the deployed OpenClaw pin still bypasses native `message:sent` for agent-main Path-B sends. Current source already routes Telegram action `sendMessage` through durable outbound delivery, which emits the internal hook. This PR adds the missing regression guard so the source behavior cannot regress back to the live-dist bypass.

## Test Plan
- `corepack pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts -t "emits internal message:sent once"`
- `corepack pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts`

Note: `corepack pnpm lint extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts` timed out in this sandbox while running the broader oxlint shard wrapper, so I ran the direct oxlint command above.
target.author: mids-neo
